text: The sourdough here is the real deal — blistered crust, open crumb, and a tang that keeps me coming back every Saturday morning.
As someone with celiac, finding Honeycomb's gluten-free line was a game changer. Their GF sandwich loaf actually holds together and tastes like real bread.
Aiden clearly cares about his craft. Watched him pull a fresh batch of seeded sourdough out of the oven and it was gone within the hour. Best bakery in Carytown.
Great morning buns and the honey lavender scone is a must. Gets crowded on weekends so come early before the sourdough sells out.
Picked up a gluten-free chocolate babka for my sister's birthday and nobody could tell it was GF. Honeycomb is a Richmond treasure.
Solid brunch spot along the river. The shrimp and grits are excellent and service was quick even on a busy Sunday.
Good vibes and a nice patio, but a little pricey for the portion sizes. Worth it once for the view.
Lamplighter roasts a fantastic single-origin pour-over. My go-to before walking down to the bakery for a loaf.
Cozy coffee shop with friendly baristas who remembered my order on the second visit. Wifi is reliable for getting work done.
Pharmacy staff are quick and helpful, and they always have my prescription ready. The pharmacist took time to answer questions about a new medication.
Best yoga studio in the area, no notes. The vinyasa flow classes are challenging and the instructors are wonderful.
Loved browsing the used fiction section. The staff recommendations are always spot on and they have a great little local authors shelf.
Well-stocked neighborhood grocery with a surprisingly good cheese counter. Staff helped me find local Virginia honey.
Classic Richmond diner — huge breakfast portions and bottomless coffee. The line at peak hours moves fast.
Nice green space for a weekend walk. Clean trails and plenty of shade. Brought a Honeycomb loaf for a picnic and it was perfect.
Great spot for the kids to run around. Could use a few more benches but otherwise a lovely neighborhood park.
Small but thoughtfully curated local history exhibits. Spent a quiet afternoon here and learned a lot about old Richmond.
Skyline Bistro nailed the date-night vibe. The seasonal tasting menu was creative and the service impeccable.
Cedar pulls a beautiful espresso and pairs it perfectly with a flaky sourdough pastry. Cozy atmosphere and the latte art is always on point.
Sugar & Twine has lovely croissants, though I still think Honeycomb has the edge on sourdough. Friendly staff and a charming Carytown corner.
